content_en:
“And when you look there (in Paradise), you will see a delight (that can not be imagined), and a great dominion’) First this chain is disconnected, there being a gap between ‘AbdurRahmaan — who is weak — and Al-Hasan. Second, it is a Mursal narration.

Abu Ayyoob related the following narration, which he ascribed to the Prophet #%, “Verily, the people of Paradise will visit one another on white superior mounts, which are like rubies. And there are no beasts in Paradise except for horses and camels.” (Related by Abu Na‘eem)

‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar 4 said, “In Paradise there are superior horses and noble camels, which the dwellers of Paradise will ride.” (Related by ‘Abdullah ibn Al-Mubaarak)

This wording does not limit how many kinds of animals there will be in Paradise, as opposed to the narration of Abu Na‘eem from Abu Ayyoob #. The narration of Abu Na’eem is contrary to what Tbn Maajah related in his Sunan, from ‘Abduilah ibn ‘Umar ts: that the Messenger of Allah #% said: “Sheep is from the animals of Paradise.” Therefore this is Munkar.

And in a narration related in the Musnad of Al-Bazzaar, the Prophet 2% said, “Treat goats kindly, and remove harm from them, for they

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 723

are from the beasts of Paradise.”

Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah related that the Prophet 2 said, “When the people of Paradise enter Paradise, horses that have wings and that are made of red rubies will come to them. They neither urinate nor drop dung. The dwellers of Paradise will sit on them, and then they will fly with them in Paradise. Then the All-Mighty (Allah 4) will appear to them, and when they see Him, they will fall down to Him, prostrating. The All-Mighty (Allah 4%) will say to them, ‘Raise your heads, for this day is not a day of work; rather, it is a day of bliss and honor. So they raise their heads, and Allah will send down rain of perfume on them. Then they will pass by dunes of musk, and then Allah will send a wind to those dunes, which will be stirred up upon them, until they will return to their wives, and they will be disheveled and dust-covered (not from dunes of dust and sand but from dunes of musk). (Related by Abu Ash-Shaikh AlAsbahaanee)

“lee # related that he heard the Messenger of Allah #8 say, “Verily, in Paradise there is a tree; from its top and its bottom horses made of gold come out, horses that are saddled and bridled with pearls and rubies, They neither urinate nor drop manure. They have wings, and orte stride of theirs is like the distance they can see before them. The dwellers of Paradise will ride them, and will fly on them to wherever they please (to go). Those below them in ranking will say, “By dint of what did your slaves reach all of this honor?’ He will say to them, ‘They would pray in the night, while you were sleeping; they would fast, while you were eating; they would spend (in charity), while you were being stingy; they would fight (in the way of Allah), while you were afraid.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

The Dwellers Of Paradise Will Visit One Another; They Will Gather And Reminisce About Their Affairs In The World, In Terms Of Acts Of Obedience And Mistakes

Allah 48 said: os oe a he phere we te a phre tthe ala & ey Bio OK gh & es Gop (4. Ho Ail ok Oy Ce a os ca

ail 3 oO oak a BG re

724 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

“And some of them draw near to others, questioning. Saying: “A foretime, we were afraid (of the punishment of Allah 3% in the midst of our families. So Allah %% has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of Fire. Verily, We used to invoke Him (Alone and none else) before. Verily, He is Al-Barr (the Most Subtle, Kind, Courteous, and Generous), the Most Merciful’)

Anas related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “When the people of Paradise enter (Paradise) and when brothers will long for one another, the bed of this one will travel to the bed of that one, until they are gathered together. One of them will say to his companion, ‘Do you know when Allah forgave us?’ His companion will say, ‘We were in such and such place, when we supplicated to Allah, and then He forgave us.” (Related by Abu Bakr Ibn Abee

SEE SBE ie) ail

“Then they will turn to one another, mutually questioning. A speaker of them will say: “Verily, I had a companion (in the world), who used to say: “Are you among those who believe (in resurrection after death). “(That) when we die and become dust and bones, shall we indeed (be raised up) to receive reward or punishment (according to our deeds)?” (The speaker) said: “Will you look down?” So he looked down and saw him in the midst of the Fire. He said: “By Allah #8! you have nearly ruined me. “Had it not been for the Grace of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth (to Hell).”” “(The dwellers of Paradise will say) Are we then not to die (any more)? “Except our first death, and we shall not be punished? (after we have entered Paradise). Truly this is

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 725

the supreme success! For the like of this let the workers work. au

“The (supreme) success” encompasses both jinns and mankind. He will say, “He (ie., my companion) used to whisper to me disbelief and the unlikelihood of the return (after death). Then by the Mercy of Allah I was saved from him.” Then his (his referring to the one who would whisper disbelief to others) companions will be ordered to look down into the Hellfire. And then they will see him being punished in its midst. His companion (who is in Paradise) will praise Allah for saving him from that (evil) companion (and from the punishment of Hell).

“He said: “By Allah 88! you have nearly ruined me. “Had it not been for the Grace of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth (to Hell),

Then he will mention the happiness and bliss that he is in, and he will thank Allah for being upon that state. And Allah #@ said:

“(The dwellers of Paradise will say) Are we then not to die (any more)? “Except our first death, and we shall not be punished? (after we have entered Paradise).

“For the like of this let the workers work’)

This might be the end of that person’s statement, or it might be from the Speech of Allah ‘Azza We-Jall (To Him belongs Might and

726 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Majesty), which is similar to what He 4 said in this Verse:

“And for this let (ail) those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten

Sulaimaan ibn Mugheerah related that Humaid ibn Hilaal said, “Tt has reached us that the higher dwellers of Paradise will visit the lower ones, but the lower dwellers of Paradise will not visit the higher ones.” This can signify one of two meanings:

First, the one of a lower ranking is not fitted to go above or beyond it; he does not qualify to go above his own station.

Second, so that the lower dweller of Paradise cannot see what the higher dweller has in terms of bliss and thus become sad in the process, for there is no sadness or grief in Paradise. What Humaid ibn Hilaal said has in fact been related in a Hadecth that is ascribed to the Prophet #8, with some additional words. Abu Umaamah . said, “The Messenger of Allah #% was asked, ‘Will the people of Paradise visit one another?” He #% said, “The higher one will visit the lower one, but the lower one will not visit the higher one, except for the ones who loved one another for Allah. They will go wherever they want on she-camels...”

Shafee ibn Maatai’ # related that the Messenger of Allah + said, “From the bliss of Paradise is that they will visit one another,

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 727

(riding) on mounts and on Al-Bukht (camels with long necks). And they will be given saddled and bridled horses in Paradise, horses that neither urinate nor drop manure. They will ride them until they reach wherever Allah ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) pleases (for them to reach). Then something like a cloud will come to them; in it is that which no eye has seen and no ear has heard. They will say, ‘Rain down upon us.’ It will continue to rain down upon them until that ends. Then Allah will send a rain that does not harm, and then... a dune of musk, from their right and from their left. That musk is found on the forelocks of their horses, and also at the parting of their hair, and on their heads, Fach man from them has a direction which his soul desires. Then the Musk attaches itself to them, to their horses, and to other than that from their garments. Then they will turn until they reach whatever place Allah ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) pleases (for them to reach). A woman calls one of those, saying, ‘O ‘Abdullah (slave of Allah), do you not have any need for us?” He will say, ‘Who are you?’ She will say, ‘Iam your wife and your love.’ He will say, ‘I do not know where you are?” She will say, ‘Do you not know that Allah said: er thy ee tot a aA me pee seer oe ChE Gh 2B i BSG oe op “No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a reward for what they used to do") He will say, ‘Yes, by my Lord’ Perhaps he will become occupied after that time, not turning around and not returning. He will not become occupied away from her except because of the blessing and honor he is in.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) This hadith is Mursal and very much Ghareeb. Abu Hurairah related that the Prophet 2% asked Jibreel 38 about this verse: OG Ai oe oreo 42 0.708 OM a be SS BA 4 5 OCA GF Se yA a> 42 1G
content_id:
'Dan ketika kamu melihat ke sana (di Surga), kamu akan melihat kenikmatan (yang tidak dapat dibayangkan), dan kerajaan yang besar.' Pertama, sanad ini terputus, ada celah antara 'Abdur-Rahman — yang lemah — dan Al-Hasan. Kedua, ini adalah riwayat Mursal.

Abu Ayyub meriwayatkan riwayat berikut, yang ia nisbahkan kepada Nabi, 'Sesungguhnya penghuni Surga akan saling mengunjungi dengan mengendarai tunggangan-tunggangan unggul berwarna putih, yang seperti rubi. Dan tidak ada binatang di Surga kecuali kuda dan unta.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Nu'aim)

'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (ra) berkata, 'Di Surga ada tunggangan-tunggangan unggul dan unta-unta mulia, yang akan ditunggangi oleh penghuni Surga.' (Diriwayatkan oleh 'Abdullah ibn Al-Mubarak)

Lafaz ini tidak membatasi berapa jenis hewan yang akan ada di Surga, berbeda dengan riwayat Abu Nu'aim dari Abu Ayyub (ra). Riwayat Abu Nu'aim bertentangan dengan apa yang diriwayatkan Ibn Majah dalam Sunan-nya, dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (ra): bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, 'Domba adalah dari binatang-binatang Surga.' Oleh karena itu ini adalah Munkar.

Dan dalam riwayat yang terdapat dalam Musnad Al-Bazzar, Nabi bersabda, 'Perlakukanlah kambing dengan baik, dan jauhkan gangguan dari mereka, karena mereka

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Tribulasi Besar 723

adalah dari binatang-binatang Surga.'

Jabir ibn 'Abdullah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda, 'Ketika penghuni Surga masuk ke dalam Surga, kuda-kuda bersayap yang terbuat dari rubi merah akan datang kepada mereka. Mereka tidak buang air kecil maupun buang air besar. Penghuni Surga akan duduk di atasnya, kemudian mereka akan terbang bersama mereka di Surga. Kemudian Yang Maha Mulia (Allah) akan menampakkan diri kepada mereka, dan ketika mereka melihat-Nya, mereka akan berjatuhan kepada-Nya, bersujud. Yang Maha Mulia (Allah) akan berkata kepada mereka, 'Angkatlah kepala kamu, karena ini bukan hari untuk bekerja; melainkan, ini adalah hari kesenangan dan kehormatan.' Maka mereka mengangkat kepala mereka, dan Allah akan menurunkan hujan wewangian kepada mereka. Kemudian mereka akan melewati gundukan-gundukan misk, kemudian Allah akan mengirimkan angin ke gundukan-gundukan itu, yang akan berhamburan ke atas mereka, hingga mereka akan kembali kepada istri-istri mereka, dalam keadaan acak-acakan dan berdebu (bukan dari gundukan debu dan pasir tetapi dari gundukan misk).' (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Asy-Syaikh Al-Asbahani)

'Ali (ra) meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah bersabda, 'Sesungguhnya di Surga ada sebuah pohon; dari puncaknya dan dari bawahnya keluar kuda-kuda dari emas, kuda-kuda yang disadel dan dikekang dengan mutiara dan rubi. Mereka tidak buang air kecil maupun buang air besar. Mereka memiliki sayap, dan satu langkah mereka seperti jarak yang dapat mereka lihat di hadapan mereka. Penghuni Surga akan menunggangi mereka, dan akan terbang di atasnya ke mana saja mereka inginkan (untuk pergi). Orang-orang di bawah mereka dalam peringkat akan berkata, 'Karena apa hamba-hamba-Mu sampai pada semua kehormatan ini?' Dia akan berkata kepada mereka, 'Mereka biasa salat di malam hari, sementara kamu tidur; mereka biasa berpuasa, sementara kamu makan; mereka biasa berinfak (dalam bersedekah), sementara kamu pelit; mereka biasa berperang (di jalan Allah), sementara kamu takut.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abi Ad-Dunya)

Para Penghuni Surga Akan Saling Mengunjungi; Mereka Akan Berkumpul dan Mengenang Urusan-Urusan Mereka di Dunia, Berupa Amal-Amal Ketaatan dan Kesalahan-Kesalahan

Allah berfirman:

724 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Tribulasi Besar

'Dan sebagian dari mereka menghampiri sebagian yang lain, bertanya-tanya. Berkata: 'Dahulu, kami merasa takut (akan azab Allah di tengah-tengah keluarga kami. Maka Allah telah melimpahkan karunia kepada kami, dan telah menyelamatkan kami dari azab api. Sesungguhnya, Kami dahulu berdoa kepada-Nya (semata dan tidak kepada yang lain). Sesungguhnya, Dialah Al-Barr (Yang Maha Halus, Lembut, Penuh Perhatian, dan Murah Hati), Yang Maha Penyayang.''

Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, 'Ketika penghuni Surga masuk (ke Surga) dan ketika para saudara akan saling merindu, tempat tidur orang ini akan berpindah ke tempat tidur orang itu, hingga mereka berkumpul bersama. Salah seorang di antara mereka akan berkata kepada temannya, 'Apakah kamu tahu kapan Allah mengampuni kita?' Temannya akan berkata, 'Kami berada di tempat ini dan itu, ketika kami berdoa kepada Allah, kemudian Dia mengampuni kami.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abi)

'Kemudian mereka akan berpaling kepada satu sama lain, saling bertanya. Seorang pembicara di antara mereka akan berkata: 'Sesungguhnya aku dahulu mempunyai seorang teman (di dunia), yang biasa berkata: 'Apakah kamu termasuk orang-orang yang membenarkan (kebangkitan setelah mati).' '(Bahwa) ketika kita mati dan menjadi debu dan tulang, apakah kita sungguh-sungguh (akan dibangkitkan) untuk menerima balasan atau hukuman (sesuai dengan amal perbuatan kita)?' (Si pembicara) berkata: 'Maukah kamu melihat ke bawah?' Maka ia melihat ke bawah dan melihatnya di tengah-tengah api Neraka. Ia berkata: 'Demi Allah! Sungguh kamu hampir membinasakanku. 'Seandainya bukan karena Karunia Tuhanku, tentulah aku termasuk orang-orang yang dihadirkan (ke Neraka).'' '(Para penghuni Surga akan berkata) Apakah kita tidak akan mati lagi? 'Kecuali kematian kita yang pertama, dan kami tidak akan diazab? (setelah kita masuk Surga). Sungguh ini adalah

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Tribulasi Besar 725

keberhasilan yang agung! Maka untuk hal yang seperti inilah hendaknya para pekerja beramal.'

'Keberhasilan (yang agung)' mencakup baik jin maupun manusia. Ia akan berkata, 'Ia (yaitu, temanku) biasa membisikkan kekufuran kepadaku dan ketidakmungkinan kembali (setelah kematian). Kemudian dengan Rahmat Allah aku diselamatkan darinya.' Kemudian teman-temannya (yang mengacu pada orang yang biasa membisikkan kekufuran kepada orang lain) akan diperintahkan untuk melihat ke bawah ke dalam Neraka. Dan kemudian mereka akan melihatnya sedang diazab di tengah-tengahnya. Temannya (yang ada di Surga) akan memuji Allah karena menyelamatkannya dari teman (yang jahat) itu (dan dari azab Neraka).

'Ia berkata: 'Demi Allah! Sungguh kamu hampir membinasakanku. 'Seandainya bukan karena Karunia Tuhanku, tentulah aku termasuk orang-orang yang dihadirkan (ke Neraka).'

Kemudian ia akan menyebutkan kebahagiaan dan kenikmatan yang sedang ia rasakan, dan ia akan bersyukur kepada Allah karena berada dalam keadaan itu. Dan Allah berfirman:

'(Para penghuni Surga akan berkata) Apakah kita tidak akan mati lagi? 'Kecuali kematian kita yang pertama, dan kami tidak akan diazab? (setelah kita masuk Surga).'

'Maka untuk hal yang seperti inilah hendaknya para pekerja beramal.'

Ini mungkin merupakan akhir dari pernyataan orang tersebut, atau mungkin ini dari Firman Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Kepada-Nya segala Kemuliaan dan

726 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Tribulasi Besar

Keagungan), yang serupa dengan apa yang Dia firmankan dalam Ayat ini:

'Dan untuk hal yang seperti inilah hendaknya semua orang berupaya yang ingin berupaya (yaitu, bersegera menuju)'

Sulaiman ibn Mughirah meriwayatkan bahwa Humaid ibn Hilal berkata, 'Telah sampai kepada kami bahwa penghuni Surga yang lebih tinggi akan mengunjungi yang lebih rendah, namun penghuni Surga yang lebih rendah tidak akan mengunjungi yang lebih tinggi.' Ini dapat berarti salah satu dari dua makna:

Pertama, orang yang berperingkat lebih rendah tidak cocok untuk naik atau melampauinya; ia tidak berhak untuk pergi di atas kedudukannya sendiri.

Kedua, agar penghuni Surga yang lebih rendah tidak dapat melihat apa yang dimiliki penghuni yang lebih tinggi berupa kenikmatan dan dengan demikian menjadi sedih dalam prosesnya, karena tidak ada kesedihan atau duka di Surga. Apa yang dikatakan Humaid ibn Hilal sebenarnya telah diriwayatkan dalam sebuah hadis yang dinisbahkan kepada Nabi, dengan beberapa kata tambahan. Abu Umamah (ra) berkata, 'Rasulullah ditanya, 'Apakah penghuni Surga akan saling mengunjungi?' Beliau bersabda, 'Yang lebih tinggi akan mengunjungi yang lebih rendah, namun yang lebih rendah tidak akan mengunjungi yang lebih tinggi, kecuali bagi mereka yang saling mencintai karena Allah. Mereka akan pergi ke mana saja yang mereka inginkan dengan mengendarai unta betina...'

Syafi' ibn Mati' (ra) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, 'Di antara kenikmatan Surga adalah bahwa mereka akan saling mengunjungi,

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Tribulasi Besar 727

(mengendarai) tunggangan-tunggangan dan Al-Bukht (unta-unta berleher panjang). Dan mereka akan diberi kuda-kuda yang disadel dan dikekang di Surga, kuda-kuda yang tidak buang air kecil maupun buang air besar. Mereka akan menungganginya hingga mereka mencapai tempat yang dikehendaki Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Kepada-Nya segala Kemuliaan dan Keagungan) (bagi mereka untuk dicapai). Kemudian sesuatu seperti awan akan datang kepada mereka; di dalamnya terdapat apa yang belum pernah dilihat mata dan belum pernah didengar telinga. Mereka berkata, 'Turunkanlah kepada kami.' Ia akan terus menurunkan hujan kepada mereka hingga itu berakhir. Kemudian Allah akan mengirimkan hujan yang tidak membahayakan, kemudian... sebuah gundukan misk, dari kanan dan dari kiri mereka. Misk itu ditemukan pada jambul kuda-kuda mereka, dan juga pada belahan rambut mereka, dan di kepala mereka. Setiap pria di antara mereka memiliki suatu arah yang diinginkan jiwanya. Kemudian Misk menempel pada mereka, pada kuda-kuda mereka, dan pada selain itu dari pakaian mereka. Kemudian mereka berbalik hingga mencapai tempat yang dikehendaki Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Kepada-Nya segala Kemuliaan dan Keagungan) (bagi mereka untuk dicapai). Seorang wanita memanggil salah seorang dari mereka, berkata, 'Wahai 'Abdullah (hamba Allah), tidakkah kamu memiliki kebutuhan untuk kami?' Ia akan berkata, 'Siapakah kamu?' Ia berkata, 'Aku adalah istrimu dan cintamu.' Ia akan berkata, 'Aku tidak tahu di mana kamu?' Ia berkata, 'Tidakkah kamu tahu bahwa Allah berfirman: 'Tidak seorang pun mengetahui apa yang disembunyikan untuk mereka berupa kesenangan sebagai balasan atas apa yang mereka kerjakan.' Ia berkata, 'Ya, demi Tuhanku.' Mungkin ia akan tersibukkan setelah waktu itu, tidak menoleh dan tidak kembali. Ia tidak akan tersibuk darinya kecuali karena berkah dan kehormatan yang sedang ia rasakan.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abi Ad-Dunya) Hadis ini adalah Mursal dan sangat Gharib. Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bertanya kepada Jibril tentang ayat ini:
content_ar: “And عندما you look there (in الجنة), you will see a delight (that can not be imagined), and a great dominion’) أولاً this chain is disconnected, there being a gap between ‘AbdurRahmaan — who is weak — and Al-حسن. ثانياً, it is a Mursal narration. Abu Ayyoob related the following narration, which he ascribed to النبي ﷺ #%, “Verily, the people of الجنة will visit بعضهم بعضاً on white superior mounts, which are like rubies. And there are no beasts in الجنة except for horses and camels.” (Related by Abu Na‘eem) ‘Abdullah ibn ‘عمر 4 said, “In الجنة there are superior horses and noble camels, which the dwellers of الجنة will ride.” (Related by ‘Abdullah ibn Al-Mubaarak) This wording does not limit how many kinds of animals there will be in الجنة, as opposed to the narration of Abu Na‘eem from Abu Ayyoob #. The narration of Abu Na’eem is contrary to what Tbn Maajah related in his Sunan, from ‘Abduilah ibn ‘عمر ts: that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said: “Sheep is from the animals of الجنة.” لذلك this is Munkar. And in a narration related in the Musnad of Al-Bazzaar, النبي ﷺ 2% said, “Treat goats kindly, and remove harm from them, for they الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 723 are from the beasts of الجنة.” Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah related that النبي ﷺ 2 said, “عندما the people of الجنة enter الجنة, horses that have wings and that are made of red rubies will come to them. They neither urinate nor drop dung. The dwellers of الجنة will sit on them, and then they will fly with them in الجنة. Then the All-Mighty (الله 4) will appear to them, and عندما they see Him, they will fall down to Him, prostrating. The All-Mighty (الله 4%) will say to them, ‘Raise your heads, for this day is not a day of work; rather, it is a day of bliss and الشرف. لذا they raise their heads, and الله will send down rain of perfume on them. Then they will pass by dunes of musk, and then الله will send a wind to those dunes, which will be stirred up upon them, حتى they will return to their wives, and they will be disheveled and dust-covered (not from dunes of dust and sand but from dunes of musk). (Related by Abu Ash-Shaikh AlAsbahaanee) “lee # related that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 say, “Verily, in الجنة there is a tree; from its top and its bottom horses made of gold come out, horses that are saddled and bridled with pearls and rubies, They neither urinate nor drop manure. They have wings, and orte stride of theirs is like the distance they can see قبل them. The dwellers of الجنة will ride them, and will fly on them to wherever they please (to go). Those below them in ranking will say, “By dint of what did your slaves reach all of this الشرف?’ He will say to them, ‘They would pray in the night, بينما you were sleeping; they would fast, بينما you were eating; they would spend (in الصدقة), بينما you were being stingy; they would fight (in the الطريق of الله), بينما you were afraid.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) The Dwellers Of الجنة Will Visit بعضهم بعضاً; They Will Gather And Reminisce About Their Affairs In The الدنيا, In Terms Of Acts Of Obedience And Mistakes الله 48 said: os oe a he phere we te a phre tthe ala & ey Bio OK gh & es Gop (4. Ho Ail ok Oy Ce a os ca ail 3 oO oak a BG re 724 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations “And some of them draw near to others, questioning. Saying: “A foretime, we were afraid (of the العقاب of الله 3% in the midst of our families. لذا الله %% has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of Fire. Verily, We used to invoke Him (Alone and none else) قبل. Verily, He is Al-Barr (the Most Subtle, Kind, Courteous, and Generous), the Most Merciful’) أنس related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “عندما the people of الجنة enter (الجنة) and عندما brothers will long for بعضهم بعضاً, the bed of this one will travel to the bed of that one, حتى they are gathered together. One of them will say to his companion, ‘Do you know عندما الله forgave us?’ His companion will say, ‘We were in such and such place, عندما we supplicated to الله, and then He forgave us.” (Related by أبو بكر Ibn Abee SEE SBE ie) ail “Then they will turn to بعضهم بعضاً, mutually questioning. A speaker of them will say: “Verily, I had a companion (in the الدنيا), who used to say: “Are you among those who believe (in البعث بعد الموت). “(That) عندما we die and become dust and bones, shall we indeed (be raised up) to receive الأجر or العقاب (وفقاً لـ our deeds)?” (The speaker) said: “Will you look down?” لذا he looked down and saw him in the midst of the Fire. قال: “By الله #8! you have nearly ruined me. “Had it not been for the الفضل of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth (to النار).”” “(The dwellers of الجنة will say) Are we then not to die (any more)? “Except our أولاً الموت, and we shall not be punished? (بعد we have entered الجنة). Truly this is الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 725 the supreme الفلاح! For the like of this let the workers work. au “The (supreme) الفلاح” encompasses both jinns and mankind. He will say, “He (ie., my companion) used to whisper to me disbelief and the unlikelihood of the return (بعد الموت). Then by the الرحمة of الله I was saved from him.” Then his (his referring to the one who would whisper disbelief to others) الصحابة will be ordered to look down into the النار. And then they will see him being punished in its midst. His companion (who is in الجنة) will praise الله for saving him from that (الشر) companion (and from the العقاب of النار). “قال: “By الله 88! you have nearly ruined me. “Had it not been for the الفضل of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth (to النار), Then he will mention the happiness and bliss that he is in, and he will thank الله for being upon that state. And الله #@ said: “(The dwellers of الجنة will say) Are we then not to die (any more)? “Except our أولاً الموت, and we shall not be punished? (بعد we have entered الجنة). “For the like of this let the workers work’) This might be the end of that person’s statement, or it might be from the Speech of الله ‘Azza We-Jall (To Him belongs Might and 726 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Majesty), which is similar to what He 4 said in this الآية: “And for this let (ail) those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten Sulaimaan ibn Mugheerah related that Humaid ibn Hilaal said, “Tt has reached us that the higher dwellers of الجنة will visit the lower ones, but the lower dwellers of الجنة will not visit the higher ones.” This can signify one of two meanings: أولاً, the one of a lower ranking is not fitted to go above or beyond it; he does not qualify to go above his own station. ثانياً, لذا that the lower dweller of الجنة cannot see what the higher dweller has in terms of bliss and وهكذا become sad in the process, for there is no الحزن or الحزن in الجنة. What Humaid ibn Hilaal said has in fact been related in a Hadecth that is ascribed to النبي ﷺ #8, with some additional words. Abu Umaamah . said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #% was asked, ‘Will the people of الجنة visit بعضهم بعضاً?” He #% said, “The higher one will visit the lower one, but the lower one will not visit the higher one, except for the ones who loved بعضهم بعضاً for الله. They will go wherever they want on she-camels...” Shafee ibn Maatai’ # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله + said, “From the bliss of الجنة is that they will visit بعضهم بعضاً, الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 727 (riding) on mounts and on Al-Bukht (camels with long necks). And they will be given saddled and bridled horses in الجنة, horses that neither urinate nor drop manure. They will ride them حتى they reach wherever الله ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) pleases (for them to reach). Then something like a cloud will come to them; in it is that which no eye has seen and no ear has heard. They will say, ‘Rain down upon us.’ It will continue to rain down upon them حتى that ends. Then الله will send a rain that does not harm, and then... a dune of musk, from their الحق and from their left. That musk is found on the forelocks of their horses, and أيضاً at the parting of their hair, and on their heads, Fach man from them has a direction which his النفس desires. Then the Musk attaches itself to them, to their horses, and to other than that from their garments. Then they will turn حتى they reach whatever place الله ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) pleases (for them to reach). A woman calls one of those, saying, ‘O ‘Abdullah (slave of الله), do you not have any need for us?” He will say, ‘Who are you?’ She will say, ‘Iam your الزوجة and your المحبة.’ He will say, ‘I do not know where you are?” She will say, ‘Do you not know that الله said: er thy ee tot a aA me pee seer oe ChE Gh 2B i BSG oe op “No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a الأجر for what they used to do") He will say, ‘Yes, by my Lord’ Perhaps he will become occupied بعد that time, not turning around and not returning. He will not become occupied away from her except لأن of the البركة and الشرف he is in.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) This الحديث is Mursal and very much Ghareeb. أبو هريرة related that النبي ﷺ 2% asked Jibreel 38 about this الآية: OG Ai oe oreo 42 0.708 OM a be SS BA 4 5 OCA GF Se yA a> 42 1G
